results.title: Pokémon Winds & Waves New Eevee Evolution Divides Gamers
results.content:
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

So far, it seems like fans won't be getting any more Pokémon Winds and Waves news in 2026. A number of high-profile insiders and leakers have suggested it'll be next year before we get another peak at the Gen 10 titles, which are officially announced for 2027 but nothing more specific behind that. Hopefully, when the games are shown off again, we'll have a nice new Eeveelution or two as well.

pokemon-winds-and-waves-tag-page-cover-art.jpg

### Pokemon Winds and Waves

Follow

Followed

Action Adventure RPG

Open-World

Released
:   2027

Developer(s)
:   Game Freak

Publisher(s)
:   The Pokemon Company, Nintendo [...] Sign in to your ScreenRant account

Pokémon Winds and Waves will be arriving in 2027, and while the first official trailer for the game confirmed the new Gen 10 starters and some returning Pokémon, we don't have all the answers yet. That's naturally led to a lot of speculation on what to expect in Winds and Waves, but perhaps no rumor has been more persistent than the one stating we're getting a new Eevee evolution for Gen 10. [...] Now, one eagle-eyed Pokémon fan believes they may have found a bit of confirmation that a new Eevee evolution, or Eeveelution, will be arriving with the Gen 10 games next year. Over on Reddit, user ForTheLastTime- shared a screenshot of official descriptions from retailer Target highlighting some of the new merchandise that will be arriving in stores as part of Target's new Pokémon collection, including an Eevee evolution camera.

results.title: Gotta Watch 'Em All: Streaming the Pokémon TV Show in 2026 Is a Total Mess, But I Think I Can Help | PCMag
results.content:
## Season 1: Indigo League

Things get confusing right out of the gate with how to deal with the start of the show. The first season, retroactively titled Indigo League, ran for 80 episodes. However, it seems to now be split into two. The first 57 episodes are marketed under The Beginning banner, while the remaining episodes are folded into Season 2 on Amazon and YouTube. For accuracy's sake, I'll be sticking to the 80-episode format here. Set in the Kanto region, you can play here in Let's Go Eevee/Pikachu, Pokopia, and the newly re-released FireRed and LeafGreen games.

 Amazon Prime Video (Episodes 1-57")), Pokémon Channel (Episodes 1-26"))

results.title: 30 Years After Pokémon Red and Blue, Japan Officially Crowns No. 1 Pokémon in Latest Rankings
results.content:
With 90 collective votes, Eevee and its evolution line are the only Pokémon that came close to challenging Pikachu, Pichu and Raichu's throne. Comparatively, third-place winners Charmader, Charmeleon and Charizard only amassed 58 votes in the ranking. Introduced in Generation I, Eevee is famous for its ability to evolve into one of eight different forms, each of which represents a uniquePokémon type. The most recent addition to the Eevee family is the fairy-type Sylveon, which debuted in Generation VI. [...] Notably, many comments submitted by "Eeveeolution" fans highlighted the appeal of all eight forms rather than any one form over the others. "No matter what they evolve into, all Eeveelutions are cute," one voter stated. Eevee's first form is equally favored and frequently appears alongside Pikachu as an ambassador for Pokémon's civic projects in Japan. As one voter pointed out, an Eevee sculpture is located immediately outside the entrance to Yokohama's City Hall.

Pokopia is available now exclusively for Nintendo Switch 2. For viewers in the U.S., Pokémon Horizons is streaming on Netflix.

results.title: Know before you GO to Pokémon GO Fest 2026: Tokyo, Chicago, and Copenhagen
results.content:
Mega Mewtwo Collection in collaboration with 100 Thieves: The collection includes hoodies, trucker hats, and t-shirts featuring Mega Mewtwo X and Mega Mewtwo Y. This collection is exclusively available at GO Fest: Chicago at both the pop-up at Grant Park and the boutique at NEWCITY.
 Pokémon GO Historical Collection: Revisit all ten GO Fest artworks in a limited-edition collection of GO Fest shirts, pins, postcards, and more!
 Pokémon GO 10th Anniversary Collection: Celebrate ten years of Pokémon GO and the joyous friendships found along the way. Artwork featuring Pikachu and Eevee is available as a challenge coin, lapel pin, and T-shirt. Limited quantities are available for preorder. [...] During your park session, Trainers can look forward to Special Research connected to the recent discovery of Zeraora, four skill-based park zones, Team GO Rocket battles, Shadow Raids, themed research, photo opportunities, and more.

Be sure to stay through the end of your park session! During the final 30 minutes, ticket-holding attendees will be able to join a massive Super Mega Raid featuring Mega Mewtwo X and Mega Mewtwo Y. The Mewtwo that Trainers can catch from these raids will have a Pokémon GO Fest 2026 Location Background, and its first Mega Level will already be unlocked. Stay tuned for more information on your assigned location by checking in-game news or the email associated with your Pokémon GO account. [...] The Raid Lover add-on grants additional Raid Passes per day during the event, as well as additional XP and Candy when completing raids.
 The Egg-thusiast add-on grants bonuses for hatching Pokémon, an increased chance at receiving 10 km Eggs from spinning PokéStops and Gyms, and 1/4 Egg Hatch Distance for Eggs placed in Incubators during the event.
 The Citywide Gameplay add-on extends the Pokémon GO Fest fun throughout the city on the extra day—or days—of your choice!
 The Premier Access add-on includes 100 Premier Battle Passes, 100 Super Incubators and more in-game items. Also get access to an exclusive Premier Access Lounge onsite with upgraded restrooms, portable charger kiosks, and lockers.
